scope: Run a medium-depth full-universe portfolio pass after the runtime fixes to recover non-zero portfolio signal without returning to runaway runtime

decision_context.prior_state: The first interactive full-universe checkpoint completed, but the harness was too thin and collapsed ensemble adoption and direct choice to zero across the board.
decision_context.decision: Increase depth moderately to 6 rounds, 4 flagship personas per type, 4 ensemble runs, and 2 ensemble personas per type while keeping the optimized runtime path and low bootstrap count.
decision_context.why_now: The goal was to find the first full-universe checkpoint that preserves interactive runtime while restoring at least some non-zero adoption and choice signal.

files_expected.purpose: Stores the medium-depth full-universe portfolio run packet.
Stores the readout derived from the medium-depth full-universe portfolio packet.
Summarizes the recovered signal, top slices, and remaining limitations of the medium harness.
Captures the parameter choice and limitations for the medium-depth portfolio checkpoint.

planned_steps: Run the full 515-domain universe with a medium interactive harness
Save the portfolio packet under research/meta
Write the checkpoint note explaining what signal came back and what is still too thin

expected_checks: The full-universe packet completes under the medium harness
Top-line ensemble adoption is no longer uniformly zero
The note records that rank order is more trustworthy than absolute magnitude and that interest-to-choice friction remains prominent

guardrails: Do not treat the medium checkpoint as a maintained final portfolio verdict
Do not refresh dashboard or export from this checkpoint
Use it as the first non-zero full-universe signal recovery, not as the terminal ranking artifact
